qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Hardcoded fallback JWT secret allows unauthenticated remote shell access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a JWT authentication bypass in server middleware and WebSocket shell access, while the advisory is shell command injection in the /api/user/git-config handler in server/routes/user.js.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The accepted finding is a hardcoded fallback JWT secret in auth middleware that enables forged authentication and shell access, not the advisory's exec()-based gitName/gitEmail interpolation in /api/user/git-config.
evidence: The advisory names server/routes/user.js lines 58-59 and the /api/user/git-config endpoint as the vulnerable sink, where gitName and gitEmail are interpolated into child_process.exec() commands.

The only final qualifying finding in .judge/input_manifest.json is default_jwt_secret_websocket_rce, whose code locations are repo/server/middleware/auth.js and authenticated shell paths in repo/server/index.js.

The session execution history shows repo/server/routes/user.js contains the advisory sink, but the qualifying finding report and PoC target JWT verification and WebSocket shell access instead of the git-config route.
